option.name: filePath
option.value: /ChatItem (snapshot).kt

option.value: /ConfigViewModel (snapshot).kt

updatedContent

option.value: /MeshServiceManager (snapshot).kt

option.value: /VoiceAssembler (snapshot).kt

option.value: /VoiceChunker (snapshot).kt

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
filePath
option.value: /VoiceMessagingSteps (snapshot).kt

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
option.value:
# GitLab CI/CD for Voicetastic — builds debug & release APKs and runs unit tests. # # Toolchain: # - JDK 21 (required by AGP 9.x / Gradle 9.x) # - Android command-line tools, platform-tools, platforms;android-36, build-tools;36.0.0 # # Cache strategy: # - Gradle caches and wrappers are cached per branch. # - Android SDK is cached globally to avoid re-downloading on every job. # # Optional CI/CD variables for signing the release APK (set as protected/masked): # ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 – base64-encoded keystore (.jks/.keystore) # ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD # ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS # ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD # If any of those are missing, the release job still runs but produces an unsigned APK.
stages: - test - build - package
variables: # --- Versions (keep in sync with app/build.gradle.kts and gradle-wrapper.properties) --- ANDROID_COMPILE_SDK: "36" ANDROID_BUILD_TOOLS: "36.0.0" ANDROID_CMDLINE_TOOLS_VERSION: "11076708" # cmdline-tools rev 13.0 # --- Paths (cacheable, must live under $CI_PROJECT_DIR for GitLab cache to pick them up) --- ANDROID_SDK_ROOT: "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.android-sdk" GRADLE_USER_HOME: "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.gradle" GRADLE_OPTS: "-Dorg.gradle.daemon=false -Dorg.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx4g -Dkotlin.compiler.execution.strategy=in-process"
# Reusable bootstrap: install JDK + Android SDK, accept licenses, install components. .android-setup: &android-setup image: eclipse-temurin:21-jdk before_script: - apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq --no-install-recommends unzip wget ca-certificates - mkdir -p "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools" - | if [ ! -d "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/latest" ]; then echo "Installing Android command-line tools…" wget -q "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-${ANDROID_CMDLINE_TOOLS_VERSION}_latest.zip" -O /tmp/cmdline-tools.zip unzip -q /tmp/cmdline-tools.zip -d "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools" mv "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools" "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/latest" rm /tmp/cmdline-tools.zip fi - export PATH="$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/platform-tools:$PATH" - yes | sdkmanager --licenses > /dev/null 2>&1 || true - sdkmanager --install "platform-tools" "platforms;android-${ANDROID_COMPILE_SDK}" "build-tools;${ANDROID_BUILD_TOOLS}" > /dev/null - chmod +x ./gradlew
# Shared cache: Gradle deps + wrapper dists + Android SDK. .cache-gradle-sdk: &cache-gradle-sdk cache: key: files: - gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties - gradle/libs.versions.toml paths: - .gradle/caches - .gradle/wrapper - .android-sdk - app/build/intermediates/aar_metadata_check policy: pull-push
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Stage: test — unit tests # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- unit-tests: stage: test <<: *android-setup <<: *cache-gradle-sdk script: - ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest artifacts: when: always reports: junit: - app/build/test-results/testDebugUnitTest/TEST-*.xml paths: - app/build/reports/tests/ expire_in: 1 week
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Stage: build — debug APK (always built, on every branch and MR) # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- assemble-debug: stage: build <<: *android-setup <<: *cache-gradle-sdk script: - ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleDebug artifacts: name: "voicetastic-debug-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" paths: - app/build/outputs/apk/debug/*.apk expire_in: 4 weeks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Stage: package — release APK (only on tags / main branch) # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- assemble-release: stage: package <<: *android-setup <<: *cache-gradle-sdk script: - | if [ -n "$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64" ] && [ -n "$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD" ] \ && [ -n "$ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS" ] && [ -n "$ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD" ]; then echo "Signing keystore detected — building signed release APK." echo "$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64" | base64 -d > "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/release.keystore" export RELEASE_STORE_FILE="$CI_PROJECT_DIR/release.keystore" export RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD="$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD" export RELEASE_KEY_ALIAS="$ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS" export RELEASE_KEY_PASSWORD="$ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD" else echo "No signing credentials provided — release APK will be unsigned." fi - ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleRelease - rm -f "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/release.keystore" artifacts: name: "voicetastic-release-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" paths: - app/build/outputs/apk/release/*.apk expire_in: 12 weeks rules: - if: '$CI_COMMIT_TAG' - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH' when: manual allow_failure: true

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# GitLab CI/CD for Voicetastic — builds debug & release APKs and runs unit tests. # # Toolchain: # - JDK 21 (required by AGP 9.x / Gradle 9.x) # - Android command-line tools, platform-tools, platforms;android-36, build-tools;36.0.0 # # Cache strategy: # - Gradle caches and wrappers are cached per branch. # - Android SDK is cached globally to avoid re-downloading on every job. # # Optional CI/CD variables for signing the release APK (set as protected/masked): # ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 – base64-encoded keystore (.jks/.keystore) # ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD # ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS # ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD # If any of those are missing, the release job still runs but produces an unsigned APK.
stages: - test - build - package
# Default runner selection. Override per-job via `tags:` if needed. # - `linux` : OS family # - `docker` : executor (matches `eclipse-temurin:21-jdk` image) # - `android`: capability tag for runners with adequate RAM/disk for AGP 9 builds # On GitLab.com SaaS, replace with: [saas-linux-medium-amd64] default: tags: - linux - docker - android
variables: # --- Versions (keep in sync with app/build.gradle.kts and gradle-wrapper.properties) --- ANDROID_COMPILE_SDK: "36" ANDROID_BUILD_TOOLS: "36.0.0" ANDROID_CMDLINE_TOOLS_VERSION: "11076708" # cmdline-tools rev 13.0 # --- Paths (cacheable, must live under $CI_PROJECT_DIR for GitLab cache to pick them up) --- ANDROID_SDK_ROOT: "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.android-sdk" GRADLE_USER_HOME: "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.gradle" GRADLE_OPTS: "-Dorg.gradle.daemon=false -Dorg.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx4g -Dkotlin.compiler.execution.strategy=in-process"
# Reusable bootstrap: install JDK + Android SDK, accept licenses, install components. .android-setup: &android-setup image: eclipse-temurin:21-jdk before_script: - apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq --no-install-recommends unzip wget ca-certificates - mkdir -p "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools" - | if [ ! -d "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/latest" ]; then echo "Installing Android command-line tools…" wget -q "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-${ANDROID_CMDLINE_TOOLS_VERSION}_latest.zip" -O /tmp/cmdline-tools.zip unzip -q /tmp/cmdline-tools.zip -d "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools" mv "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools" "$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/latest" rm /tmp/cmdline-tools.zip fi - export PATH="$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/platform-tools:$PATH" - yes | sdkmanager --licenses > /dev/null 2>&1 || true - sdkmanager --install "platform-tools" "platforms;android-${ANDROID_COMPILE_SDK}" "build-tools;${ANDROID_BUILD_TOOLS}" > /dev/null - chmod +x ./gradlew
# Shared cache: Gradle deps + wrapper dists + Android SDK. .cache-gradle-sdk: &cache-gradle-sdk cache: key: files: - gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties - gradle/libs.versions.toml paths: - .gradle/caches - .gradle/wrapper - .android-sdk - app/build/intermediates/aar_metadata_check policy: pull-push
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Stage: test — unit tests # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- unit-tests: stage: test <<: *android-setup <<: *cache-gradle-sdk script: - ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testDebugUnitTest artifacts: when: always reports: junit: - app/build/test-results/testDebugUnitTest/TEST-*.xml paths: - app/build/reports/tests/ expire_in: 1 week
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Stage: build — debug APK (always built, on every branch and MR) # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- assemble-debug: stage: build <<: *android-setup <<: *cache-gradle-sdk script: - ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleDebug artifacts: name: "voicetastic-debug-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" paths: - app/build/outputs/apk/debug/*.apk expire_in: 4 weeks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Stage: package — release APK (only on tags / main branch) # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- assemble-release: stage: package <<: *android-setup <<: *cache-gradle-sdk # Heavier job — prefer a beefier runner if you label one. # On SaaS, swap to: [saas-linux-large-amd64] tags: - linux - docker - android script: - | if [ -n "$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64" ] && [ -n "$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD" ] \ && [ -n "$ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS" ] && [ -n "$ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD" ]; then echo "Signing keystore detected — building signed release APK." echo "$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64" | base64 -d > "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/release.keystore" export RELEASE_STORE_FILE="$CI_PROJECT_DIR/release.keystore" export RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD="$ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD" export RELEASE_KEY_ALIAS="$ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS" export RELEASE_KEY_PASSWORD="$ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD" else echo "No signing credentials provided — release APK will be unsigned." fi - ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:assembleRelease - rm -f "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/release.keystore" artifacts: name: "voicetastic-release-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" paths: - app/build/outputs/apk/release/*.apk expire_in: 12 weeks rules: - if: '$CI_COMMIT_TAG' - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH' when: manual allow_failure: true

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
filePath
option.value: /mesh (snapshot).proto

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
filePath
option.value: /messaging (snapshot).feature

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
Feature: Text Messaging As a Meshtastic user I want to send and receive text messages over the mesh network So that I can communicate with other nodes
Background: Given the Meshtastic service is connected And my node ID is "!mynode01"
Scenario: Send a broadcast text message Given no destination node is selected When I send a text message "Hello mesh!" Then the message should be sent to broadcast And the message "Hello mesh!" should appear in the chat as outgoing
Scenario: Send a direct text message to a specific node Given the destination node "!target01" is selected When I send a text message "Hey there!" Then the message should be sent to "!target01" And the message "Hey there!" should appear in the chat as outgoing
Scenario: Receive a text message When a text message "Hi from mesh" is received from "!remote01" Then the message "Hi from mesh" should appear in the chat as incoming And the message should show sender "!remote01"
Scenario: Send empty message is ignored When I send a text message "" Then no message should be sent
Scenario: Send message when disconnected fails Given the Meshtastic service is disconnected When I send a text message "This won't work" Then no message should be sent
Scenario: Channel conversation shows broadcast messages but not DMs to other nodes Given no destination node is selected When a broadcast message "Hello all" is received from "!remote01" And a direct message "Private hello" is received from "!remote01" to "!mynode01" Then the channel conversation should contain "Hello all" And the channel conversation should contain "Private hello"
Scenario: Channel conversation excludes outgoing DMs Given no destination node is selected And the destination node "!remote01" is selected When I send a text message "DM to remote" Given no destination node is selected Then the channel conversation should not contain "DM to remote"
Scenario: DM conversation only shows messages between me and the selected node Given the destination node "!remote01" is selected When a direct message "Hey you" is received from "!remote01" to "!mynode01" And a broadcast message "Hello everyone" is received from "!remote01" And a direct message "Not for me" is received from "!remote02" to "!mynode01" Then the DM conversation with "!remote01" should contain "Hey you" And the DM conversation with "!remote01" should not contain "Hello everyone" And the DM conversation with "!remote01" should not contain "Not for me"
Scenario: Sent DM appears in DM conversation Given the destination node "!remote01" is selected When I send a text message "Direct to you" Then the DM conversation with "!remote01" should contain "Direct to you"
Scenario: Messages on different channels are separated Given no destination node is selected And the selected channel is 0 When a broadcast message "On channel 0" is received from "!remote01" on channel 0 And a broadcast message "On channel 1" is received from "!remote01" on channel 1 Then the channel conversation should contain "On channel 0" And the channel conversation should not contain "On channel 1"
Scenario: Switching channel shows correct messages Given no destination node is selected When a broadcast message "Primary msg" is received from "!remote01" on channel 0 And a broadcast message "Secondary msg" is received from "!remote01" on channel 1 And the selected channel is 1 Then the channel conversation should contain "Secondary msg" And the channel conversation should not contain "Primary msg"
Scenario: Send message on selected channel Given no destination node is selected And the selected channel is 2 When I send a text message "Channel 2 message" Then the message should be sent on channel 2

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
Feature: Text Messaging As a Meshtastic user I want to send and receive text messages over the mesh network So that I can communicate with other nodes
Background: Given the Meshtastic service is connected And my node ID is "!mynode01"
Scenario: Send a broadcast text message Given no destination node is selected When I send a text message "Hello mesh!" Then the message should be sent to broadcast And the message "Hello mesh!" should appear in the chat as outgoing
Scenario: Send a direct text message to a specific node Given the destination node "!target01" is selected When I send a text message "Hey there!" Then the message should be sent to "!target01" And the message "Hey there!" should appear in the chat as outgoing
Scenario: Receive a text message When a text message "Hi from mesh" is received from "!remote01" Then the message "Hi from mesh" should appear in the chat as incoming And the message should show sender "!remote01"
Scenario: Send empty message is ignored When I send a text message "" Then no message should be sent
Scenario: Send message when disconnected fails Given the Meshtastic service is disconnected When I send a text message "This won't work" Then no message should be sent
Scenario: Channel conversation shows only broadcast messages Given no destination node is selected When a broadcast message "Hello all" is received from "!remote01" And a direct message "Private hello" is received from "!remote01" to "!mynode01" Then the channel conversation should contain "Hello all" And the channel conversation should not contain "Private hello"
Scenario: Channel conversation excludes outgoing DMs Given the destination node "!remote01" is selected When I send a text message "DM to remote" Given no destination node is selected Then the channel conversation should not contain "DM to remote"
Scenario: DM conversation only shows messages between me and the selected node Given the destination node "!remote01" is selected When a direct message "Hey you" is received from "!remote01" to "!mynode01" And a broadcast message "Hello everyone" is received from "!remote01" And a direct message "Not for me" is received from "!remote02" to "!mynode01" Then the DM conversation with "!remote01" should contain "Hey you" And the DM conversation with "!remote01" should not contain "Hello everyone" And the DM conversation with "!remote01" should not contain "Not for me"
Scenario: Sent DM appears in DM conversation Given the destination node "!remote01" is selected When I send a text message "Direct to you" Then the DM conversation with "!remote01" should contain "Direct to you"
Scenario: Messages on different channels are separated Given no destination node is selected And the selected channel is 0 When a broadcast message "On channel 0" is received from "!remote01" on channel 0 And a broadcast message "On channel 1" is received from "!remote01" on channel 1 Then the channel conversation should contain "On channel 0" And the channel conversation should not contain "On channel 1"
Scenario: Switching channel shows correct messages Given no destination node is selected When a broadcast message "Primary msg" is received from "!remote01" on channel 0 And a broadcast message "Secondary msg" is received from "!remote01" on channel 1 And the selected channel is 1 Then the channel conversation should contain "Secondary msg" And the channel conversation should not contain "Primary msg"
Scenario: Send message on selected channel Given no destination node is selected And the selected channel is 2 When I send a text message "Channel 2 message" Then the message should be sent on channel 2
